prompt_text: Open source or affordable customer support tools instead of Drift
response:
# Open Source & Affordable Customer Support Alternatives to Drift

## Open Source Options

### **Chatwoot**
- Free, self-hosted live chat & ticketing
- Multi-channel (chat, email, social media, WhatsApp)
- Team collaboration features
- Community-driven development
- [chatwoot.com](https://www.chatwoot.com)

### **Tiledesk**
- Open-source live chat & chatbot platform
- Self-hosted or cloud
- Conversation management, knowledge base
- Omnichannel support
- [tiledesk.com](https://tiledesk.com)

### **Zulip Server**
- Self-hosted team communication
- Better for internal + customer support workflows
- Topic-based threading
- [zulip.com](https://zulip.com)

---

## Affordable Proprietary Tools

### **Budget-Friendly**
- **Zendesk Chat** (~$15/month) - Basic live chat
- **Intercom** (free tier available) - Limited but functional
- **Help Scout** (~$20/month) - Email-based support, lightweight
- **Freshchat** (~$10/month) - Lightweight live chat
- **Crisp** (~$25/month) - Simple, clean interface

### **Freemium with Good Free Tiers**
- **Supabase** + custom build - For DIY approach
- **Firebase** + Dialogflow - Chatbot foundation
- **HubSpot Service Hub** (free tier) - Basic ticketing

---

## Hybrid Approach
**Combine**: Open-source chat (Chatwoot) + ticketing system (Plane/Focalboard) = $0-50/month hosting

**Best for:** Startups wanting control and affordability

What's your main use case—live chat, helpdesk, or both?
